intro.text: Like an arrow, you are released with certainty,
your eyes fixed on the mark
never failing, never straying from the prey you choose.
Like the darkness that settles without warning,
you are quiet and unsettling,
casting fear into others
while a far greater fear coils within your own chest.
Like a tranquil sea, you draw me in
and I stand torn
between surrendering to your depths
or watching you from the safety of the shore,
wary of what sleeps beneath your calm.
You are a sovereign in your own mind
a king of pride and unyielding command,
claiming all things as though they were born beneath your crown.
Yet do not mistake my silence for obedience, my dear.
If you are the king swollen with pride,
then I am the one who defies your throne.
If you are the night that swallows the sky,
I am the first light of dawn
slipping through the door of hope
to end your reign of shadows.
If you are the quiet sea that threatens in stillness,
then I am the wind that will rouse you
that will summon waves and life back into your waters.
Do not blame yourself for failing to understand me;
you have never known my name.
Call me the storm, if you must
the force that will overturn your world
and break the spine of your arrogance.
You will change.
I will change you
with or without your consent,
my magnificent,
stubborn pride.

chapter1.text: In a vast mansion in the district of Maadi, a handful of sharp cries echoed through the halls.
Omniyah’s voice rang again, impatient and rising.
Sabah finally answered, appearing at the doorway.

chapter1.speaker: Omniyah
chapter1.text: Do you not have ears? I’ve been calling you all morning.

chapter1.speaker: Sabah
chapter1.text: I am sorry, Madam, I was just

chapter1.speaker: Omniyah
chapter1.text: and go call the young master upstairs.

chapter1.speaker: Omniyah
chapter1.text: If his father finds him still asleep,

chapter1.speaker: Omniyah
chapter1.text: he will bring this whole villa down on our heads.
Omniyah, mother of our protagonist, was a distinguished lady of high society in her early forties.
Yet no one would guess her age at first glance;
her face remained tender and youthful, framed by soft silken hair.
Still, do not be fooled by such beauty.
In her world, nothing mattered but wealth and appearances,
and nothing repulsed her more than those of the middle or lower class
whom she saw as a burden upon society.
Sabah knocked gently on the door upstairs,
fearful of what mood awaited her on the other side.

chapter1.speaker: Sabah
chapter1.text: Master Nader… Master Nader, please wake up.

chapter1.speaker: Sabah
chapter1.text: If your father finds you asleep,
A deep voice responded, thick with irritation.

chapter1.speaker: Nader
chapter1.text: let me change and I’ll come down.
Under her breath, Sabah murmured to herself,

chapter1.speaker: Sabah
chapter1.text: You were once the pride of this house,

chapter1.speaker: Sabah
chapter1.text: an example to all young men.

chapter1.speaker: Sabah
chapter1.text: May God take justice from those who ruined you.
Inside the room, Nader lay sprawled across his bed.
He had grown used to long nights with his so-called friends,
hauling himself up the stairs only to continue drinking alone.
with dark, smooth hair, amber eyes, and an athletic build.
he had been the national boxing champion.
But life does not hold its course for long.
wincing from the ache pounding inside his skull.

chapter1.speaker: Nader
chapter1.text: Better get dressed and disappear before they start again.
He reached for his phone, dialing Shady
the man he called his friend.
Yet a true friend is one who guides you when you lose your way,
who reminds you of who you are when you forget.
But Nader was blind to that truth,
and Shady was all he saw.

chapter1.speaker: Shady
chapter1.text: Your mood will change in no time.

chapter1.speaker: Nader
chapter1.text: I love you most when you have everything set.

chapter1.speaker: Shady
chapter1.text: Everyone’s waiting for the king to show up.
Nader threw on a white shirt beneath a navy suit,

chapter1.speaker: Nader
chapter1.text: the most beautiful woman in the world.

chapter1.speaker: Omniyah
chapter1.text: Aren’t you tired of this disgraceful behavior?

chapter1.speaker: Nader
chapter1.text: I don’t need this lecture now

chapter1.speaker: Nader
chapter1.text: I need to leave before Father returns

chapter1.speaker: Omniyah
chapter1.text: You cannot keep this up, Nader.
A commanding voice echoed from behind them.

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: It is all your doing, Madam.

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: A useless boy like you wakes at six in the evening
Fawzy al-Damanhouri   father of our protagonist
was the owner of the largest chain of travel companies in the Middle East.
A traditional man, strict with his son,
hoping one day Nader would return to who he once was.
he cared little for social divisions

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: All of this is your fault

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: yours and that wretched woman who ruined him.

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: My son was once a man,

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: for a worthless woman who toyed with you?
Nader took a step toward the door,
but his father’s voice pinned him in place.

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: with that garbage you call friends?

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: they’ll leave you in the street

chapter1.speaker: Nader
chapter1.text: I do not force anyone to love me.

chapter1.speaker: Nader
chapter1.text: I am not begging the world to stay.

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: As you sow, so shall you reap.

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: and she suffers what you once did?

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: Not every woman is like Haidy.
A flash of anger twisted Nader’s features.

chapter1.speaker: Nader
chapter1.text: Do not speak her name again.

chapter1.speaker: Nader
chapter1.text: So this is how it is.

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: If you walk out that door,

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: I will strip you of everything.

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: this house will no longer be yours.

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: waste your life with drunken fools,

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: and spend it on them again.

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: You are drowning yourself in trash, boy

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: what will you say to God?
A single tear escaped Nader’s eye
as he walked away in silence.

chapter1.speaker: Fawzy
chapter1.text: You never cared for either of us, Omniyah.

chapter2.text: and drove as fast as he could,
He drove to the Nile Corniche
until the sun rose above him.
Let’s leave Nader for a moment...
A woman in her early forties
allowing the sunlight to flood inside
in the lives of this small family
the woman who dedicates her life

the beauty of her light hazel eyes
but what made her stand out the most

more like a sister to her.
and walked into the living room

chapter2.speaker: Maya

chapter2.speaker: Adham
chapter2.text: Did I give birth to you?

chapter2.speaker: Adham
chapter2.text: What kind of nonsense is that?

chapter2.speaker: Maya
chapter2.text: And since you love her so much,

chapter2.speaker: Maya

chapter2.speaker: Adham

Maya quickly hid behind her father.
the father of Maya and Adham,

chapter2.speaker: Adel
chapter2.text: Seeing you grow up like this

chapter2.speaker: Adel
chapter2.text: As if I could say otherwise.

chapter2.speaker: Samira

A shadow of sorrow crossed his face.

chapter2.speaker: Adel
chapter2.text: Every time I look at the kids,

chapter2.speaker: Adel

chapter2.speaker: Samira

They continued talking for a while.

chapter2.speaker: Maya
chapter2.text: if you keep daydreaming like that.

chapter2.speaker: Maya
chapter2.text: and let’s go pick her up.

chapter2.speaker: Maya
chapter2.text: Hope whoever hates me gets cursed.

chapter2.speaker: Maya

chapter2.speaker: Nada

Maya pulled Nada beside her on the back seat.

chapter2.speaker: Maya
chapter2.text: Let’s go save humanity at the hospital.
He sat silently by the Nile,
leaning his head back as memories from a year ago slowly resurfaced.
Nader had once been an extremely respectable man.
He worked in one of his father’s companies,
but he had made a firm decision:
he wanted to build himself with his own effort,
not merely as the son of a wealthy businessman.
he worked tirelessly until he became the head of one of the companies,
and eventually his father handed him complete control over it.
Nader was excellent at his job.
He wanted to create a name for himself even greater than his father’s.
Fouzy El-Damanhory was proud of him.
Nader was also committed to his prayers and deeply disciplined…
a storm arrived that shattered his ocean of success,
There had been an opening for a new secretary after his previous one resigned for personal reasons.
A young woman entered the company
and long silky hair flowing down her back.

chapter2.speaker: Haidy
chapter2.text: Excuse me, where’s Mr. Nader’s office?
The receptionist looked at her sharply.

chapter2.speaker: Receptionist
chapter2.text: Third floor. First office in front of you.
She headed upstairs searching for Nader El-Damanhory’s office
Nader noticed the unfamiliar voice and looked up.

chapter2.speaker: Haidy
chapter2.text: I’m Haidi Azzam, sir. Your new secretary.

chapter2.speaker: Nader
chapter2.text: and Mr. Mohamed will explain everything to you.
Nader found himself staring at her angelic face for a moment.
but he quickly pushed them away.
He had never cared much about women
his only focus had always been work.
Every day, Haidi slowly cast her nets around Nader,
convincing him with her fake politeness and fabricated innocence.
In reality, she only wanted his money.
Her intentions were as ugly as her greed.
But eventually, Nader fell for her beauty and her carefully crafted manners.
One day, he finally decided to confess his feelings.

chapter2.speaker: Nader
chapter2.text: Haidi, could you come into my office for a moment?
Moments later, she knocked and entered.

chapter2.speaker: Nader
chapter2.text: Yes… There’s something I want to ask you, but please don’t refuse.

chapter2.speaker: Nader
chapter2.text: Would you let me take you out to lunch after work today?

chapter2.speaker: Nader
chapter2.text: I want to talk to you about something.

chapter2.speaker: Haidy
chapter2.text: I’m sorry, sir, but I can’t.

chapter2.speaker: Haidy
chapter2.text: We should only discuss work inside the company during work hours.

chapter2.speaker: Nader
chapter2.text: Wait. I’m sorry if I upset you.

chapter2.speaker: Haidy
chapter2.text: It’s just not appropriate for us to go somewhere alone when there’s no relation between us.
Nader looked at her with admiration.

chapter2.speaker: Nader
chapter2.text: God, Haidi… you’re such a respectable woman.

chapter2.speaker: Nader
chapter2.text: I’m really sorry if my words offended you. But I truly need to talk to you.

chapter2.speaker: Haidy
chapter2.text: Alright, sir. But please, I can’t stay out long.
After work, Nader and Haidi headed to the place they had agreed on.
